dialogue_line_replacements.text: Elegiría España. Estudiaría flamenco y viajaría por toda Europa. ¡Me mola la idea!
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: I would choose Spain. I would study flamenco and travel all over Europe. I love that idea! (Spain: molar = to like)

dialogue_line_replacements.text: Escogería el mandarín. Con inglés, español y chino, ¡podría comunicarme con la mitad del mundo! ¡Qué pasada!
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: I would choose Mandarin. With English, Spanish and Chinese, I could communicate with half the world! Wow! (Spain: ¡qué pasada! = how amazing!)

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: In Spain, the conditional is used identically to Latin America. '¿Podría...?' is equally polite. 'Debería' is widely used for suggestions.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: Spain-specific expressions with conditional: '¿Querías algo?' (did you want something? — conditional used as past in some regions). '¿Me podrías decir...?' is very common for polite questions in Spanish daily life.
